name: Chocolate Pecan-Fudge Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup unsalted butter (1 stick), at room temperature
1 cup light brown sugar, firmly packed
1/4 cup sour cream, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Lightly grease a 9-inch round cake pan.
3. With an electric mixer on medium speed, beat butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
4. With the mixer on medium-low speed, beat in eggs until well blended.
5. Add syrup, Bailey's, coffee granules, and flour; mix until well combined.
6. Fold in chopped pecans.
7. Pour batter into prepared pan.
8. Bake for 55 to 60 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
9. Remove from cake pan to a wire rack to cool.
(GLAZE)
1. Melt chocolate in a small heavy saucepan over very low heat.
2. Dip one end of each whole pecan into melted chocolate; place on waxed paper and refrigerate until chocolate has hardened.
3. To the remaining melted chocolate, stir Bailey's until well blended, 1 tablespoon at a time.
4. Place cooled cake on a serving plate. Spread glaze over top of cake.
5. Decorate cake with whole pecans.
6. Cut into wedges to serve.
Makes 10 to 12 servings.
